expectations.text: Uses aws-iam auth type (not universal-auth with client-id/secret)
expectations.evidence: Auth config shows 'auth: type: "aws-iam"' with 'identity-id' field, not universal-auth

expectations.text: Shows correct auth config with identity-id field
expectations.evidence: Config shows 'identity-id: "<your-machine-identity-id>"' with comment 'Paste the ID from Step 1'

expectations.text: Shows ECS task definition pattern with shared volume between agent and app containers
expectations.evidence: Complete ECS task definition with two containers (infisical-agent and app) sharing 'secrets' volume with mountPoints specified for both

expectations.text: Uses correct template function (listSecrets or listSecretsByProjectSlug) with proper signature
Mentions exit-after-auth option for init-style sidecar pattern
expectations.evidence: Config shows 'exit-after-auth: true' with comment 'Render once and exit' and separate section 'Optional: Continuous Secret Updates' that shows setting to false

expectations.text: Does not require manually managing any credentials or access keys
expectations.evidence: Uses AWS IAM authentication through ECS task role - no explicit credential files or keys needed to be managed
